Blavatnik School of Government · Brand Typography

Font comparison and alternatives

Side-by-side review of current brand fonts, internal document fonts, and recommended alternatives — with diacritic coverage testing.

Note on Brandon Text: Brandon Text is a licensed commercial font and cannot be loaded in this HTML preview. Brandon Text is now loaded directly from your uploaded font files. All other fonts are loaded live from Google Fonts.
Current fonts
What BSG uses today
Brandon Text (external/web) and Century Gothic (internal Word documents — bundled with Microsoft Office on both Windows and Mac). Open Sans is used alongside Brandon Text on the website.
Brandon Text
HVD Fonts · Commercial licence

Option A
Replace Century Gothic for internal Word documents
A better companion to Brandon Text for memos, reports and internal templates. Must be pre-installed or available via Microsoft 365 cloud fonts — no download burden on staff.

Option B
Replace Brandon Text entirely with one free font
A single font family for web, external documents and internal documents. Must feel similar to Brandon Text (geometric, warm, clean), have zero licensing friction, and cover Latin Extended Additional glyphs including Yoruba (ọ, ẹ, ṣ), Vietnamese, Turkish and more.

Reading test
Long-form paragraph comparison
How each font performs at body text sizes, approximating a research paper abstract, event listing, or staff profile page.
Brandon Text (current brand font)
Government, Markets and Regulation
MPP Core Course · Michaelmas Term
This course examines the relationship between government policy and market outcomes, with particular attention to regulatory design in contexts of institutional fragility. Drawing on cases from Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia and Latin America, students will analyse how policy choices interact with political economy constraints.
Convened by Professor Ẹniọlá Ànúolúwapọ́ Ṣóyẹmí and Dr Thị Nguyễn. Guest speakers will include senior officials from the OECD, World Bank and national governments.
8 weeks · 16 hours contact time · Assessment by policy memo
Avenir Next LT Pro · Licence required for web

Summary
Full comparison table
All fonts scored across the criteria that matter for BSG's use case.
Font Cost Brandon similarity Diacritics (ext. Latin) Web use Word / pre-installed Notes
Brandon Text (current) Licensed — ⚠ Partial ✓ ✗ No Missing Yoruba / W. African glyphs
Open Sans (current web) Free Low ✓ Good ✓ ⚠ Some Good fallback. Less distinctive.
Century Gothic (current internal) Pre-installed Medium ✗ Poor ✗ ⚠ Mac only Bundled with Office on Windows and Mac. Not a Windows system font.
Avenir Next LT Pro (single-font candidate) Paid (Monotype) Very high ⚠ Partial ⚠ Licence req. ⚠ M365 cloud Closest to Brandon. Same geometric DNA. Web licence needed from Monotype/Adobe Fonts.
Jost* ★ Single-font candidate Free High ✓ Excellent ✓ ⚠ Download req. Free Futura/Avenir hybrid. Very geometric. More character than most free alternatives.
Urbanist ★ Closest to Brandon Free Very high ✓ Excellent ✓ ⚠ Download req. Possibly the closest free font to Brandon in warmth and refinement. Geometric with subtle humanist touches.
Raleway Free Medium ✓ Good ✓ ⚠ Download req. Elegant and distinctive. More personality than most. Lighter feel at thin weights.
Internal — with character
Jost* ★ With character Free High ✓ Excellent ✗ ⚠ Download req. Free Futura/Avenir hybrid. Very geometric. More personality than Microsoft defaults.
Internal — Microsoft defaults
Aptos ★ Internal rec Free (M365) Medium ✓ Good ✗ ✓ Win 11 / M365 Microsoft's new standard. Forward-looking.
Gill Sans Nova Free (M365 cloud) High ⚠ Partial ✗ ⚠ M365 cloud Most Brandon-like feel. Needs cloud fonts enabled.
Poppins ★ Single-font rec Free Very high ✓ Excellent ✓ ⚠ Download req. Best Brandon substitute. Widely used in policy sector.
Plus Jakarta Sans Free High ✓ Excellent ✓ ⚠ Download req. More distinctive than Poppins. Slightly more personality.
DM Sans Free Medium ✓ Very good ✓ ⚠ Download req. Cleaner, more neutral. More institutional in feel.
Nunito Sans Free High ✓ Excellent ✓ ⚠ Download req. Warmer/rounder than Poppins. Slightly less formal.
Noto Sans Free Low ✓ Best-in-class ✓ ✓ Wide Use as CSS fallback behind Brandon Text for now.
Quick action: Add font-family: 'Brandon Text', 'Noto Sans', sans-serif; to your site CSS today to fix diacritic rendering on people pages immediately, without waiting for the redesign.
Architectural connection
The geometric O
Brandon Text was chosen in part because its capital O is a near-perfect circle — a direct echo of the Blavatnik School building's circular drum geometry. This tests how closely each alternative preserves that circular quality versus introducing optical corrections (flattening, squaring, or stress angles) that would break the architectural reference.
The original — and why it's not a contender
Futura (Paul Renner, 1927)
Every font on this page is ultimately a descendant of Futura — the geometric sans-serif that defined the circular O as a typographic ideal. Brandon Text, Avenir, Century Gothic, Jost, Poppins — all draw directly from its 1927 design. It is the purest expression of the geometric principle that connects BSG's typography to the circular geometry of the School building.

However, Futura is not a realistic candidate for BSG's use: Not available in Office. Futura is absent from Windows, macOS system fonts, and Microsoft 365 cloud fonts entirely — ruling it out as an internal document font and making a single-font strategy unworkable for most staff.

Poor diacritic coverage. Designed in 1927, its glyph set predates the extended Latin characters BSG needs. The ọ, ẹ, ṣ problem would be worse than Brandon Text.

Expensive web licensing. Futura web licensing through Linotype/Monotype is significantly more costly than other options, with no free or open-source version available.

Designed for display, not body text. At smaller sizes Futura can feel cold and hard to read — which is precisely why Brandon Text was developed as a more legible derivative. The fonts on this page should be understood as modern, practical alternatives that preserve Futura's geometric spirit while solving the problems it cannot.

OXFORD
Government Policy
○ Humanist oval
Why this matters: Brandon Grotesque (the original signage font) and Brandon Text both use a geometric, optically near-circular O — part of the same Futura-derived tradition as Gill Sans and Century Gothic. Of the free alternatives, Poppins comes closest to preserving this quality, making it the strongest like-for-like replacement. Plus Jakarta Sans and Nunito Sans introduce a slight oval at heavier weights. Open Sans, being a humanist sans, departs most from the circular ideal.
